---
title: "Mouse modelling data"
subtitle: Autumn 2025
date: last-modified
format: 
  html:
    self-contained: true
    theme: cosmo
    fontcolor: black
    fig-width: 8  
execute:
  echo: false
  warning: false
  message: false
editor: source
toc: true
---

```{r}
#| label: setup
#| message: false
#| include: FALSE

## load packages
library(targets)
library(dplyr)
library(purrr)
library(mvgam)
library(tibble)
library(gt)
library(scales)    # for col_bin()
library(ggplot2)
#library(tidyr)
#library(lubridate)
#library(ggh4x)
#library(sf)

## load targets 
targets::tar_load(
  c(model_data,
    aez_adj)
)


```



```{r}
#| label: covs_cor

## 1. compute correlation matrix (pairwise complete obs)
#corr_mat <- model_data %>%
#  select(
#    soil_moisture_lag, rainfall_lag, evapotranspiration_lag,
#    frost_days_lag, heat_days_lag, degree_days_lag
#  ) %>%
#  cor(use = "pairwise.complete.obs") %>%
#  round(digits = 2)
#
## 2. replace exact 1.0 (within a small tolerance) with NA
#tol <- 1e-8
#corr_mat[abs(corr_mat - 1) < tol] <- NA
#
## 3. df for gt
#corr_df <- as.data.frame(corr_mat) %>%
#  rownames_to_column("Variable")
#
## 4. build the gt table
#corr_df %>%
#  gt() %>%
#
#  # colour the cell backgrounds (na.color sets background for NAs)
#  data_color(
#    columns = -Variable,
#    colors = col_bin(
#      bins = c(-1, -0.5, 0.5, 1),
#      palette = c("darkred", "white", "limegreen"),
#      na.color = "lightgrey"
#    )
#  ) 
```



## Plots by state, region, farmer and site


```{r}
#| label: plot_by_subsite
#| results: asis

# Sort data for consistent heading order
plot_data <- model_data %>%
  arrange(state, region, farmer, site, subsite)

# Global time range so all plots share the same x-axis scale
time_range <- range(model_data$time, na.rm = TRUE)

# Iterate over states
walk(unique(as.character(plot_data$state)), function(st) {

  cat("\n## ", st, "\n\n")
  st_data <- filter(plot_data, as.character(state) == st)

  # Iterate over regions within state
  walk(unique(as.character(st_data$region)), function(reg) {

    cat("\n### ", reg, "\n\n")
    reg_data <- filter(st_data, as.character(region) == reg)

    # Iterate over farmers within region (skip NA farmer rows)
    walk(na.omit(unique(as.character(reg_data$farmer))), function(farm) {

      cat("\n#### ", farm, "\n\n")
      farm_data <- filter(reg_data, as.character(farmer) == farm)

      # Iterate over sites within farmer
      walk(unique(as.character(farm_data$site)), function(si) {

        cat("\n##### ", si, "\n\n")
        site_data <- filter(farm_data, as.character(site) == si)

        message("Plotting: ", reg, " / ", farm, " / ", si)

        # One plot per site, faceted by subsite, coloured by survey method
        p <- site_data %>%
          filter(!is.na(mice_prop)) %>%
          ggplot(aes(x = time, y = mice_prop, colour = survey_method, group = series)) +
          geom_line(linetype = "dashed", alpha = 0.3) +
          geom_point() +
          scale_x_continuous(limits = time_range) +
          scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 1)) +
          facet_wrap(~subsite, ncol = 1) +
          labs(x = "Time", y = "Mice proportion", colour = "Survey method") +
          theme_bw() +
          theme(legend.position = "bottom", strip.background = element_blank())

        print(p)
        cat("\n\n")  # reset markdown context after figure output
      })
    })
  })
})

```
